sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.123 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: A finite group is called a$D_{$-group\/} if any two of its maximal$$-sub\-groups are conjugate. [r]{a)} Is it true that for any finite$D_$-group$G$and a$$-Hall subgroup$H$of$G$, there are elements$x,y,z G$such that$O_{ }(G)=H H^x H^y H^z$?
